name: sgs/form-field-radio
title: SGS Radio Field
description: A radio button group for single selection.

label.label: Group label
label._note: Renders as <legend class="sgs-form-field__label"> (not a plain <label> — this block groups multiple radio buttons under one <fieldset>). No per-instance colour/font-size/weight attribute exists; the legend is styled entirely by shared framework CSS shared with the other five form-field siblings. clusters: [] is a genuine gap, not hidden.

option.label: Radio option
option._note: Each entry in `options` renders as a repeated <label class="sgs-form-field__option"><input type="radio"><span> row — the repeater replaces the single 'input' element the text/email/textarea/select siblings have, since radio is inherently multi-node. No per-instance style attribute exists for the option row; styling is shared framework CSS. clusters: [] is a genuine gap.

help.label: Help text
help._note:
helpText itself is field-behaviour content, not a style property (Bean's non-cluster rule) — the rendered

also carries no per-instance style attribute of its own. clusters: []. The conformance checker's orphan scanner reports helpText as an ORPHAN here (its element-key-as-fallback-prefix convention coincidentally matches help+Text); this is a known false-positive of the checker, not evidence of an unaccounted style control — helpText is intentionally content, not style, and out of scope to silence without editing the checker script.

error.label: Error message
error._note: Populated by client-side validation JS, not a block attribute; the <span class="sgs-form-field__error"> has no per-instance style attribute. clusters: [].

attributes.label: How did you hear about us?
